item.title: This is Boyd Kramer, Boyd is a convicted rapist
item.description: In 2022, Kramer was found guilty of raping Ms Lane after she visited his Mosman apartment. Kramer was facing up to 14 years jail, but in a decision which has shocked the community, Judge John North handed the former water polo player just 300 hours of community service meaning he would not spend a night behind bars. https://www.news.com.au/lifestyle/real-life/news-life/mosman-rapist-boyd-kramer-unmasked/news-story/08717910b8bd334edc25ab504dca726f
item.dc:creator: MickTaylorVRussellCoight

item.title: Hell hound
item.description: It's an actual POV for once
item.dc:creator: airwoman

item.title: There's NOWHERE in America like the Salton Sea
item.description:
For better or for worse. The sea, which is the largest body of water in the U.S. outside the Great Lakes and the Great Salt Lake, was created by accident in the early 20th century when an attempt to divert a portion of the Colorado River went awry. The depression that forms the lake bed has existed for eons, back when Baja CA extended up to the same latitude as Santa Barbara.
At first it was treated like a wonderland, a new Palm Springs but with Watersports instead of Golf. Entrepreneurs sprung up everywhere.
So many dreams dashed, so many fortunes lost
Towns sprung up around the sea, like the North Shore, Salton City or Bombay Beach
Bombay Beach, from above. There's a berm on the waterfront, as the sea can rise unexpectedly and flood the town, as has happened more than once.
Of all of the towns, Bombay Beach has become the most emblematic of the hardy spirit of the lunatics who eke out a life near the slow motion ecological disaster that is the Salton Sea.
The Sea should never should have existed. There's no incoming freshwater, only polluted agricultural runoff. So if you stock it with fish, they die in massive fishkills. Slowly, the water got more & more salinized, to the point where it was saltier than the Pacific, and actually approaching Dead Sea levels of salinity. It was re-stocked with Tilapia, a Saltwater fish, but they died just as swiftly.
The stench is unbearable. Sometimes from the dead fish but more often from the Algae Blooms that rot in the stratospheric heat.
It's a living nightmare
When the sea DOES flood, it claims the abandoned surroundings and sinks them into the silt. The combination of salt air and brutal sun make the environment wildly unhospitable in contrast to the vision of Southern California being some desirable climate paradise.
The dreams that have lived and died here are uncountable.
And the pollution only gets worse.
Scientists say if the sun ever does drink the sea dry that the surrounding Coachella Valley (home of the music festival, and Palm Springs/Palm Desert plus many many farms) will become a giant swirling dust bowl.
A couple years ago it was discovered that the greatest untapped Lithium resource in the U.S. lay beneath the Salton Sea. Lithium is widely utilized in Batteries for electric powered vehicles and homes and the fortune under the Sea is unquantifiable.
Work has begun to mine the ecological nightmare. But it's not the only activity around the Sea.
A few years ago, an art festival organically developed on the eastern shores of the Sea.
Seen by many as "Burning Man without the Money," the Bombay Beach Biennale doesn't promote their starting date or really try to advertise at all. It's for locals and those in the know, and the artwork (like this wreck of a pirate ship being reclaimed by the desert) is inspired by the ruined town that hosts it.
The images are beautiful but all of them should remind you of the loss and tragedy among the beauty
The idea of home in the middle of people losing their homes
The idea of community in a place that is falling apart
And the idea of love in a place that has seen precious little of it.
The artwork springs up once a year
In installations large and small
And it cries out
Save Me
S. O. S.
item.dc:creator: Jonson71

item.title: November 20 - Transgender Day of Remembrance
item.description: November 20 - Transgender Day of Remembrance This day is dedicated to honoring the memory of transgender individuals who were murdered due to transphobia, while raising awareness about the violence our community faces. Unfortunately, given current international trends, the situation seems unlikely to improve for minorities, especially for transgender people. "No one should have to choose between surviving and being themselves." — Marsha P. Johnson
item.dc:creator: KurayFR31

item.title: I painted this oil painting of this little dog!
item.description: I painted it on commission! Both me and my client are very happy with how it came out! If you'd like to commission a painting or a drawing from me as well, contact me via my official website: anisjerbi.com Also, join my free live drawing lesson here at 5pm ET today: https://www.anisjerbiartist.com/registration-webinar
item.dc:creator: AnisJerbi

item.title: It's too late. Time for harm reduction.
item.description:
https://bsky.app/profile/chaosbuni.bsky.social/post/3lbeulj75t22p
I am fully not expecting to live a year in this regime. We have no means of escape. So my goal is to be heard and maybe get others to fight back while protecting my community as much as I can. If you do want to try and help me and my spouse escape the country, you can do so by donating at the link below, but I fully don't expect it and honestly not even sure I want it because we ALL are in danger now. I'd much rather you spend your energy on the points above than on me and my spouse escaping. https://ko-fi.com/chaosbuni
item.dc:creator: chaosbuni

item.title: What Duolingo doesn't teach you, I will
item.description: Source : https://youtube.com/shorts/1-k71jcoOmE One of my friend insisted on me teaching then insults in French (my native language) so I made this video for them. I thought this was very educational and needed to be shared with more people. Enjoy the lesson and let me know what you'd like me to teach you next :) Have a good day, Yuzu
item.dc:creator: yuzupyon

item.title: Oh Stewardess? I speak jive.
item.description: In the 1980 movie "Airplane", Barbara Billingsley was handed a script that told her to speak Jive. Not knowing how, she went to lunch with the two black actors in the scene, Al White & Norman Alexander Gibbs. The three of them improvised Jive during the whole scene.
item.dc:creator: LogicDude

item.title: Black Nazis are shocked, SHOCKED to not be picked for Orange Shitler's government. Meanwhile Stephen Miller is probably thinking about restarting the Back to Africa movement.
item.description: Faces, meet leopards. Tim Scott, Candace Owens, Ben Carson, Mark Robinson, Herschel Walker, Byron Donalds, and Daniel Cameron—all prominent supporters of Shitler, many of whom campaigned for him—will learn nothing from this, and will continue to kiss his shit-stained ass, in the hopes that he will grace them with a fat government paycheck and influence.

item.title: Hello usersub, I'm feeling bad
item.description: Posting a random picture, but I want to ask if anyone remembers a story where some guy was shaken because he was held for the first time in his adult life. I didn't find it, but I've been thinking about it a lot lately, because of reasons. Can someone share it? (edit: found it, link in comment)
item.dc:creator: xurs

item.title: A special "Kermit the Frog" themed dump in honor of Wednesday
item.description:
https://youtu.be/YRPBUeVOimU?t=18
Sadly, the song lost to some forgettable melody from "Norma Rae"
Henson sadly passed away 10 days after this special aired.
from a 1986 TV special "The Muppets: A Celebration of 30 Years", featuring all of Jim Henson's creations singing along
Belafonte said of the episode: "I seldom do television appearances, but I have always had the highest regard for Jim Henson's taste and artistry, so when I was invited to be a guest on The Muppet Show, I saw that this might be an opportunity to do something very worthwhile. I had a long talk with Jim in California at about the time he was beginning work on The Muppet Movie, and -- given that I have a more than casual interest in Third World music -- I proposed to him that the show might provide the occasion to take a look at the lore and history of other worlds, other places"
Belafonte honored Henson by performing the song again at his memorial in 1990. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G9Em3vVwsm0
item.dc:creator: Pasquinel

item.title: For Car Meet, I Present a Handful of the Cars of Antarctica
item.description:
Gotta lead with the fabled Ivan the Terra Bus
Trux w Trax If I'm driving down here, it's probably one of these guys
Shuttle Vans. As much as I make the joke about Van 404 not being found, it's probably the van I photograph the most
The Fire Dept. Ambulance getting serviced at the VMF
A Kiwi Hilux and South Pole Traverse (SPoT) tractor parked outside Skua - the station's "free thrift store"
Gotta include the Hagglunds. these things are a staple around here for getting around on the sea ice
The Kiwis have Hagglundses too
The Pisten Bully is another Sea Ice staple
Gunter Tax
item.dc:creator: VosperOfAntarctica
